questions.question: Define spanning tree. State and prove that a spanning tree of a graph with n vertices has exactly n-1 edges.
keyPoints: Definition: subgraph containing all vertices, connected, acyclic
Proof by induction on number of vertices
Base case: 1 vertex, 0 edges
Inductive step: removing a leaf gives tree with n-1 vertices and n-2 edges
Therefore tree with n vertices has n-1 edges

questions.question: For a given graph, construct the DFS spanning tree and BFS spanning tree starting from a specified vertex. Compare the two trees.
keyPoints: Perform DFS from the start vertex, record tree edges
Perform BFS from the start vertex, record tree edges
DFS tree is typically deeper (tall and narrow)
BFS tree is typically wider (short and broad, gives shortest paths)
Both have exactly V-1 edges
Identify non-tree edges (back edges for DFS, cross edges for BFS)

questions.question: Prove that adding any non-tree edge to a spanning tree creates exactly one cycle.
keyPoints: In a spanning tree, there is exactly one path between any two vertices
Adding edge (u,v) where u and v are already connected via a tree path
The new edge plus the existing path form exactly one cycle
No other cycle can be created because the rest of the tree structure is unchanged

questions.question: What is Cayley's formula? How many spanning trees does a complete graph K4 have? Enumerate them.
keyPoints: Cayley's formula: Kn has n^(n-2) spanning trees
K4 has 4^2 = 16 spanning trees
Systematic enumeration or Prufer sequence method
Each spanning tree has exactly 3 edges (n-1 = 4-1 = 3)

questions.question: Explain the properties of spanning trees. What happens when an edge is removed from or added to a spanning tree?
keyPoints: Properties: connected, acyclic, V-1 edges, unique path between any pair
Removing an edge: disconnects tree into exactly 2 components
Adding a non-tree edge: creates exactly one cycle
Spanning tree is both maximally acyclic and minimally connected
